calendar.matters:
PETITION Petition For A Court Order Affirming Revocation Of 2007 Power Of Attorney
Off Calendar May-19-2014 Off Calendar

PETITION Petition For A Court Order Affirming Revocation Of 2007 Power Of Attorney
Off Calendar Nov-06-2013 Continued To May-19-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Field for Mamita Primicias.

Ms. Field requested a one year status hearing stating that the Petitioner's Agent filed objection to the bankruptcy, and a complaint for non-dischargeability and elder financial abuse in the Bankruptcy Court which may go to trial in 9 to 12 months. Ms. Field also stated that there is concern about truthfulness in the bankruptcy matter, that they have found more takings of Mr. Ysmael and that her Client now has to be moved to an assisted living facility. The Petition for Court Order Affirming Revocation of 2007 Power of Attorney, etc. was continued to May 19, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Field is to file a status report at least three court days prior to the hearing date.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. (Not Reported)

PETITION Petition For A Court Order Affirming Revocation Of 2007 Power Of Attorney
Off Calendar Aug-28-2013 Continued To Nov-06-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Field for M. Primicias. Kathleen Lusher (Agent for M. Primicias) also present.

Ms. Field requested a two month continuance stating that Arthur Ysmael filed Chapter 7 bankruptcy, and that the Treasury Department and the Bankruptcy Trustee are doing a full investigation. The Petition for a Court Order Affirming Revocation of 2007 Power of Attorney, etc. continued to November 6, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Field is to file a status report as to what has been decided and what is still pending.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Loos. (Not Reported)

PETITION Petition For A Court Order Affirming Revocation Of 2007 Power Of Attorney
Off Calendar Jun-05-2013 Continued To Aug-28-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Field for Mamita Primicias. Roger Meredith for and with Arturo Ysmael (Objector).

The Court stated that it needs to know at what point Mr. Ysmael exercised his Power of Attorney (POA) when he transferred monies. Ms. Field stated that she was told by an Agent at Bank of America that Mr. Ysmael used his POA to gain access to an account that was in her Client's name only and transferred it to a joint account on December 31, 2012. Mr. Meredith stated that the account that is in dispute was opened in 2006 and that his Client disputes that all of those funds are Mamita's. Ms. Field stated that this account originated with a Union Bank check in the amount of $120,000.00, that the records do not know show where the money is, and that they do not have access to those records because the account records were sent to Mr. Ysmael only. Mr. Meredith stated that accounts were established prior to his Client's POA and raised the issue of whether the Court has authority to require accountings on those bank accounts. Ms. Field explained what items she received and stated that they do not have keys to the Condo, the garage door opener, no bills or tax records. The Court stated that it needs more detail as to dates when the accounts were moved, a memorandum regarding facts, points and authorities as to what power the Court has to require accountings for the joint accounts, which accounts were established prior to the Power of Attorney and which accounts were opened with the Power of Attorney. The Court ordered that the Respondent (Mr. Ysmael) provide information that was ordered by the Court on May 8, 2013 by Friday June 7, 2013 at 5:00 p.m. If the Respondent does not comply, Ms. Field may file an ex parte petition for sanctions. The Petition for a Court Order Affirming Revocation of 2007 Power of Attorney was continued to August 28, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on discovery and for setting of an evidentiary hearing.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

PETITION Petition For A Court Order Affirming Revocation Of 2007 Power Of Attorney
Off Calendar May-06-2013 Continued To Jun-05-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:Nancy Field for Mamita Primicias. Roger Meredith for and with Arturo Ysmael.

Ms. Field reported that she is having a difficult time enforcing the order unfreezing the bank account with Bank of America, that Bank of America informed her that Mr. Ysmael has moved the funds into an account with his name only. Ms. Field submitted a revised order with specific bank account numbers to unfreeze the account (order approved as to form by Mr. Meredith/order for the same signed in open Court). Mr. Meredith stated that he has no objections to the order unfreezing the account, no objections confirming revocation and validity of new powers of attorney, and no objections to delivery of all records (proposed order regarding said issues presented in open court for review and signing). The issue of breach of fiduciary duty, damages, etc. is continued to June 5, 2013.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Nancy Bautista/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

PETITION Petition For A Court Order Affirming Revocation Of 2007 Power Of Attorney
Off Calendar Apr-15-2013 Continued To May-06-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Arthur Ysmael in Properia Persona. Nancy Field for M. Primicias.

Mr. Ysmael stated the he needs time to obtain an attorney. Ms. Field stated that she has a problem with a continuance, that the elder abuse action went away after filing of her petition and that Mr. Ysmael's attorney dropped him. Ms. Field also informed the Court that Mr. Ysmael also filed an elder abuse petition and conservatorship petition in Solano County, that were both dismissed on April 5, 2013 and the Mr. Ysmael was sanctioned $4,500.00. The Petition for a Court Order Affirming Revocation of 2007 Power of Attorney was continued to May 06, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court ordered to unfreeze the assets in Bank of America to Ms. Field's client (counsel to prepare a form of order).

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Nancy Bautista/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.
